heading: Seizure, quarantine, and disposal
label: 8306 Seizure, quarantine, and disposal

children.label: A
children.text: is moving or has been moved in interstate commerce or has been imported and entered; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary has reason to believe may carry, may have carried, or may have been affected with or exposed to any pest or disease of livestock at the time of movement or that is otherwise in violation of this chapter;
any animal or progeny of any animal, article, or means of conveyance that is moving or is being handled, or has moved or has been handled, in interstate commerce in violation of this chapter;
any animal or progeny of any animal, article, or means of conveyance that has been imported, and is moving or is being handled or has moved or has been handled, in violation of this chapter; or

any animal or progeny of any animal, article, or means of conveyance that the Secretary finds is not being maintained, or has not been maintained, in accordance with any post-importation quarantine, post-importation condition, post-movement quarantine, or post-movement condition in accordance with this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: hold, seize, treat, apply other remedial actions to, destroy (including preventative slaughter), or otherwise dispose of, any animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance if the Secretary determines the action is necessary to prevent the dissemination of the pest or disease; and

children.label: B
children.text: prohibit or restrict the movement or use within a State, or any portion of a State of any animal or article, means of conveyance, or facility if the Secretary determines that the prohibition or restriction is necessary to prevent the dissemination of the pest or disease.

children.label: i
children.text: the Governor or an appropriate animal health official of the State; or1So in original. Opening quotation marks probably should not appear.1

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance under the jurisdiction of an Indian tribe, the head of the Indian tribe.1

children.label: i
children.text: notify the Governor, an appropriate animal health official of the State, or head of the Indian tribe of the proposed action;

children.label: ii
children.text: issue a public announcement of the proposed action; and

children.label: I
children.text: the findings of the Secretary;

children.label: II
children.text: a description of the proposed action; and

children.label: III
children.text: a statement of the reasons for the proposed action.

children.heading: Notice after action
children.label: C Notice after action
children.text: If it is not practicable to publish in the Federal Register the information required under subparagraph (B)(iii) before taking action under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall publish the information as soon as practicable, but not later than 10 business days, after commencement of the action.

children.heading: Quarantine, disposal, or other remedial action
children.label: c Quarantine, disposal, or other remedial action

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in writing, may order the owner of any animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance referred to in subsection (a) or (b) to maintain in quarantine, dispose of, or take other remedial action with respect to the animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance, in a manner determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: seize, quarantine, dispose of, or take other remedial action with respect to the animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance under subsection (a) or (b); and

children.label: B
children.text: recover from the owner the costs of any care, handling, disposal, or other remedial action incurred by the Secretary in connection with the seizure, quarantine, disposal, or other remedial action.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), the Secretary shall compensate the owner of any animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance that the Secretary requires to be destroyed under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), the compensation shall be based on the fair market value, as determined by the Secretary, of the destroyed animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance.

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of the destroyed animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any compensation received by the owner from a State or other source for the destroyed animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance.

children.heading: Reviewability
children.label: C Reviewability
children.text: The determination by the Secretary of the amount to be paid under this subsection shall be final and not subject to judicial review or review by any officer or employee of the Federal Government other than the Secretary or the designee of the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: any animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance that has been moved or handled by the owner in violation of an agreement for the control and eradication of diseases or pests or in violation of this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: any progeny of any animal or article, which animal or article has been moved or handled by the owner of the animal or article in violation of this chapter;

children.label: C
children.text: any animal, article, or means of conveyance that is refused entry under this chapter; or

children.label: D
children.text: any animal, article, facility, or means of conveyance that becomes or has become affected with or exposed to any pest or disease of livestock because of a violation of an agreement for the control and eradication of diseases or pests or a violation of this chapter by the owner.
